questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the British military response to the 1857 rebellion in Lucknow, which of the following officers led the initial forces before the arrival of Sir Colin Campbell?

options.text: Sir John Lawrence and Sir Henry Lawrence
Sir Henry Havelock and Sir James Outram
Sir Charles Napier and Sir Hugh Rose
Sir Robert Clive and Sir Eyre Coote

questions.explanation: According to the records of the 1857 rebellion in Lucknow, the initial British forces were led by Sir Henry Havelock and Sir James Outram. They were later followed by a stronger force under the command of Sir Colin Campbell.
questions.takeaway: The British recapture of Lucknow involved a sequence of commanders, starting with Havelock and Outram, followed by Campbell.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: English officers Sir Henry Havelock and Sir James Outram led initial forces, followed by a stronger force under Sir Colin Campbell.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the administrative evolution of Oudh under British rule, which of the following events occurred last?

options.text: The return of Oudh to governance under a Chief Commissioner.
The combination of the offices of Lieutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces and Chief Commissioner of Oudh.
The merger of Lucknow into the newly formed United Provinces of Agra and Oudh.
The formal dropping of the title of Chief Commissioner of Oudh.

questions.explanation: According to the administrative timeline: Oudh returned to British rule under a Chief Commissioner immediately after 1857 (Option A). In 1877, the offices of Lieutenant-Governor and Chief Commissioner were combined (Option B). In 1901, Lucknow was merged into the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh (Option C). Finally, in 1902, the title of Chief Commissioner was formally dropped (Option D).
questions.takeaway: The administrative consolidation of Oudh was a multi-stage process spanning from the end of the 1857 rebellion until the early 20th century.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: 1877: Offices of lieutenant-governor of the North-Western Provinces and chief commissioner of Oudh were combined. 1901: Lucknow... was merged into the newly formed United Provinces of Agra and Oudh. 1902: Title of chief commissioner was dropped with the formation of the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the administrative reorganization of North India in 1877, which of the following changes was implemented by the British government?

options.text: The separation of the judicial and executive powers of the Chief Commissioner of Oudh.
The merger of the office of Chief Commissioner of Oudh with the Lieutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces.
The relocation of the capital of the North-Western Provinces from Agra to Lucknow.
The abolition of the princely state status of Oudh following the 1857 rebellion.

questions.explanation: In 1877, the British combined the administrative leadership of two regions by merging the offices of the Lieutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces and the Chief Commissioner of Oudh.
questions.takeaway: The year 1877 marked a significant step in the administrative integration of Oudh with the North-Western Provinces.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: 1877: Offices of lieutenant-governor of the North-Western Provinces and chief commissioner of Oudh were combined.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs regarding the administrative history of Lucknow:

1. 1857: Crowning of Birjis Qadra
2. 1877: Combination of Lieutenant-Governor and Chief Commissioner offices
3. 1901: Formation of the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh

Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched based on the text. Birjis Qadra was crowned during the 1857 rebellion. In 1877, the offices of the Lieutenant-Governor of the North-Western Provinces and the Chief Commissioner of Oudh were combined. In 1901, the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh was formed, incorporating Lucknow.
questions.takeaway: The administrative history of Lucknow is defined by its transition from a rebel center to a key component of the United Provinces through specific institutional mergers.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: In the subsequent Indian Rebellion of 1857... Birjis Qadra... was crowned ruler. 1877: Offices of lieutenant-governor of the North-Western Provinces and chief commissioner of Oudh were combined. 1901: Lucknow... was merged into the newly formed United Provinces of Agra and Oudh.
